changes.description: Existence check of current Section 5 source assets. No code changes. Investigation reads cluster contract, DTO, SSOT, and current frontend rendering. Produces written decision artifact only.
changes.root_cause: v6 paper lines 358-379 (docs/strategy/HealthIQ_Final_Results_Journey_Recommendation_Paper_v6.md) classifies Section 5 as Phase 2, gated behind a formal existence check. Sprint R-7 (v6 paper lines 1049-1070) is defined as a research-and-specification sprint, not an implementation sprint. No code modifications are authorised.

cluster_ssot.naming_quality: generic internal labels only — 'Metabolic biomarkers (glucose, HbA1c, insulin)', 'Cardiovascular risk biomarkers'. No user-facing classification or governed naming fields.

current_section_5_component.note: FE-R5 comment labels this as Section 5. This component is an educational explainer ('How to understand your results') — three static blocks. It is NOT the pattern-card layer described in v6 paper Section 5. The pattern-card layer does not yet exist as a frontend component.

current_cluster_display.note: Existing cluster display component. Shows name, severity, description, biomarkers, score, confidence, recommendations, systemEducationalExplainer. Uses generic cluster names. Not a governed pattern-card layer.

changes.pre_investigation_evidence_summary: Source evidence confirms: (1) no taxonomy classification field exists anywhere in the cluster contract chain; (2) no clinical_display_name, plain_english_subtitle, or why_it_matters fields exist; (3) current cluster names are generic internal labels; (4) no governed interpretation-display layer exists. Investigation should formally document and confirm these findings. Option B (contract/content sprint first) is the strongly indicated outcome, but sprint must reach and document this conclusion formally.
changes.scope_constraint: RESEARCH AND SPECIFICATION ONLY. Sprint produces a written decision artifact. No code changes, no DTO additions, no new frontend components, no new backend logic.

stage_2b_behavioural_hardening.change_type_note: change_type declared as BEHAVIOUR. Actual sprint output is documentation only (written decision artifact). BEHAVIOUR is over-classified vs the work (which is CONTENT). Over-classification is conservative and not a BLOCKER. However: if any code modification occurs during sprint execution, that is a scope violation and Cursor must STOP.
stage_2b_behavioural_hardening.scope_defined: CONFIRMED — prompt has explicit in-scope (existence check, verification, recommendation) and out-of-scope lists (no frontend implementation, no new backend logic, no new DTO fields)
stage_2b_behavioural_hardening.no_implementation_drift_protected: CONFIRMED — prompt states 'This is NOT a frontend implementation sprint' at line 11
stage_2b_behavioural_hardening.stop_conditions: Implicit via out-of-scope list: no frontend implementation, no new backend logic, no DTO fields, no renaming, no new components. Adequate for a research sprint.
stage_2b_behavioural_hardening.intelligence_core_interaction: READ-ONLY investigation. No modification to cluster engine, DTO, or analytics pipeline.
stage_2b_behavioural_hardening.verdict: Behavioural scope is well-contained. Sprint is gating-only.

stop_conditions_verified: Do not implement Section 5 frontend — explicitly out of scope
Do not add new backend logic — explicitly out of scope
Do not add new DTO fields — explicitly out of scope
Do not rename internal contracts — explicitly out of scope
Do not build Gemini narrative — explicitly out of scope
Do not build UI prototypes — explicitly out of scope
Do not modify any existing frontend component
Do not modify any of: backend/ssot/, backend/core/pipeline/, backend/core/analytics/, backend/scripts/
